evidence_sentence: C4H::F5H transgenic lignin was comprised of as much as 97.5% syringyl units; the syringyl level in the wild-type control was 68%
notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence_sentence: Line 64 exhibits ca. 94 mol%, line 82 exhibits ca. 85 mol%, and the wild type exhibits ca. 65 mol% of syringyl lignin units, as determined by thioacidolysis
notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence_sentence: Ectopic expression of AtF5H cDNA in transgenic tobacco and poplar induces an increase in S-units in lignins
notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence_sentence: Analysis of poplar transformed with a C4H-F5H construct demonstrated significant increases in chemical (kraft) pulping efficiency; decreases of 23 kappa units and increases of more than 20 ISO brightness units in trees exhibiting high syringyl monomer concentrations
notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence_sentence: 70SOMT-3 line: 10-fold reduction in beta-O-4-linked S units (60% to 6%); almost complete lack of syringyl units
notes: COMT1 is the syringyl-specific methylation step; silencing -> loss of S units

evidence_sentence: ASOMT antisense lines: 65-67% G vs 29-31% S (vs 33-34% G; 64-67% S in control)
notes: COMT1 is the syringyl-specific methylation step; silencing -> loss of S units

evidence_sentence: The reduction in lignin content was the result of a decrease in both guaiacyl and syringyl lignins (both reduced proportionally)
notes: CCoAOMT1 KD reduces total lignin but leaves S/G ratio largely unchanged

evidence_sentence: CCR transcript levels down to 3 to 4% of wild-type; S/G ratio of FAS13 and FS40 lignin was significantly lower than that of wild type; relatively more reduced in syringyl than in guaiacyl units
notes: CCR RNAi preferentially lowers S units in poplar (Leple 2007)

evidence_sentence: Null CCR2 allele combined with haploinsufficient allele alters lignin composition
notes: Null CCR2 allele + haploinsufficient allele (De Meester 2020)

evidence_sentence: Neither the lignin amount nor the lignin monomeric composition (syringyl/guaiacyl) were significantly modified
notes: CAD acts on both coniferaldehyde and sinapaldehyde; KD does not shift S/G ratio

evidence_sentence: up to approximately 20% p-hydroxyphenyl (H) lignin subunits; increase in H monomers came at the expense of only guaiacyl (G); S maintained
notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence_sentence: C3H downregulation reduced lignin levels by half; G/S went from 0.59 WT to 0.28 C3H (so S/G from ~1.7 to ~3.6)
notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence_sentence: the S/G ratio in DEL-CK was 2.82, while the S/G ratio for DEL-C3H was 2.48
notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence_sentence: S/G ratios declined to as little as 1.1 to 1.4 in event 712 as compared with 1.8 to 2.3
notes: 4CL antisense lowers total lignin and S/G

evidence_sentence: pyMBMS analysis detected a 30% decrease in S/G ratio; 34% reduction of S-units and a small (5%) reduction of G-units
notes: 4CL1 CRISPR KO preferentially reduces S relative to G (Wang 2020)

evidence_sentence: CRISPR-KO of 4CL5 did not change either lignin content or S/G ratio
notes: 4CL5 CRISPR KO does not change S/G (4CL5 is not the lignin-dedicated paralog)

evidence_sentence: S/G ratio decreased from 2.06 in the wild type to 1.85 and 1.9 in hpCSE#1 and hpCSE#2
notes: CSE1 RNAi modestly reduces S/G (Saleme 2017)

evidence_sentence: CRISPR double KO of CSE1 and CSE2 shows importance and partial redundancy in lignification
notes: Both paralogs CRISPR-edited (de Vries 2021); effective full KO of CSE function

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of PtrWND2 and PtrWND6 in Arabidopsis leads to activation of secondary wall biosynthetic genes and ectopic deposition of secondary walls
notes: SND1 OE activates secondary wall program; modest S/G increase via MYB/F5H1 route

evidence_sentence: Dominant repression of PtrWND2B reduced wall thickness of xylary fibers by 56%
notes: Dominant repression creates a chimeric repressor on SND1-family targets; treated as composite KO

evidence_sentence: thickness of secondary walls in wood fibers in PtrMYB3 and PtrMYB21 repressors was reduced by 60 to 70%
notes: Master-switch MYB dominant-repression: 60-70% wall-thickness reduction

evidence_sentence: strong alterations in wall thickness and vessel morphology in wood of stems
notes: Master-switch MYB dominant-repression: strong secondary-wall alteration

evidence_sentence: 24% and 18% increase in lignin for 35S:PtrMYB152 and PtrCesA8:PtrMYB152; S/G monomer ratio higher (0.37-0.42) vs wild-type (0.31)
notes: MYB152 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence_sentence: Transgenic plants overexpressing PtoMYB156 displayed a dramatic decrease in secondary wall thicknesses of xylem fibers and the content of cellulose, lignin and xylose compared with wild-type
notes: MYB156 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence_sentence: 35S:PdMYB221: lignin decreased 28%, cellulose 10%, xylose 26%; plants cannot remain upright
notes: MYB221 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence_sentence: No significant changes in lignin content; 5-7% increase in S/G ratios
notes: KNAT7 OE: slight S/G increase (Ahlawat 2021)

evidence_sentence: significant decrease of about 6% lignin; 8-12% increase in S/G ratios
notes: KNAT7 antisense also gives slight S/G increase (KNAT7 is a modulatory brake; both OE and KD shift composition slightly)

evidence_sentence: Expression of a phosphorylation-null mutant version of LTF1 led to stable protein accumulation and persistent attenuation of lignification in wood cells
notes: LTF1 is a poplar MYB repressor regulated by phosphorylation; no direct ortholog node in the network

evidence_sentence: S/G lignin ratio was increased in PtNF-YA11-overexpressing poplars
notes: NF-YA11 activates F5H; OE raises S/G (Wei 2024)

evidence_sentence: CRISPR-Cas9 edited nf-ya11 poplars exhibit a decrease in S-lignin composition and S/G ratio
notes: nf-ya11 poplars show reduced S-lignin and S/G ratio (Wei 2024)

evidence_sentence: S/G ratio of 2.2 compared with 2.1 in WT. The difference was not significant
notes: Ptr-miR397a OE lowers total lignin via laccase suppression; S/G unchanged (Lu 2013)

evidence_sentence: Dominant repression of PtrWND6B reduced wall thickness by 68%
notes: SND1-B (WND6B) DR gave 68% wall reduction; collapsed to SND1 composite

evidence_sentence: lignin content of HCT samples was 9.4% lower than non-modified samples; no significant differences in S/G ratio and the distribution of the inter-unit linkages between the control and HCT down-regulated poplar
notes: HCT RNAi lowers total lignin without shifting S/G (Peng 2014; Zhou 2018)

evidence_sentence: In the wild-type poplar, the lignin in tension wood contained a significantly higher percentage of syringyl than normal wood or opposite wood
notes: Tension-wood lignin is S-enriched relative to normal/opposite wood (Al-Haddad 2013)

evidence_sentence: Wild-type Populus tremula x alba stem xylem: ~33-34% G, 64-67% S; S/G ratio ~2.0
notes: Negative control: no modifications, expected=unchanged

evidence_sentence: S/G ratio of 2.1 in WT Populus trichocarpa stem xylem
notes: Negative control for the reference species

evidence_sentence: Arabidopsis wild-type controls showed S/G 0.31 vs 0.37-0.42 in 35S:PtrMYB152 lines
notes: Negative control for heterologous Arabidopsis assays (MYB152/MYB221 OE)
